subject: Computer Networking
title: HTTP/2 and Multiplexing
description: A micro-lesson that makes HTTP/2 and Multiplexing usable.

hook.text: Web pages need dozens of files: HTML, CSS, JS, images. HTTP/1.1 loaded them mostly one at a time.

buildup.text: HTTP/2 introduced multiplexing — many requests and responses travel over a single TCP connection.

discovery.text: Streams are interleaved. While waiting for a big image, smaller CSS files slip through the same connection.

twist.text: HTTP/2 also compresses headers and allows server push — sending resources before the browser asks.

climax.text: Most major websites run on HTTP/2 today. Users don't see it, but pages load noticeably faster.

punchline.text: HTTP/2 sends more through less. One connection, many streams.

quiz.question: What is multiplexing in HTTP/2?
options: Sending multiple requests over a single connection simultaneously
Splitting a file into packets for faster delivery
